title: ClarAIty Knowledge System
description: Complete implementation of codebase knowledge DB with scan, query, visualize, and task management capabilities.

title: Configurable Subagents - Feature Epic
description:
Users can create, view, edit, and delete custom subagents through the VS Code extension sidebar. Built-in subagents are read-only but can be forked. Config persists to .clarity/agents/*.md files. Agent hot-reloads subagents after changes.

Scope decisions for POC:
- Project-scoped only (.clarity/agents/) for now
- UI form (name, description, system prompt, tools) + "open in editor" escape hatch
- Built-ins: read-only, forkable
- Custom: full CRUD
- Hot reload on save
summary: POC complete. All subtasks closed: Python CRUD handlers (list/save/delete/reload), subagent loader priority logic, hot-reload + tool description refresh, TypeScript protocol types, React SubagentsPanel with checkbox tool selection, sidebar-provider message routing, tests (Python CRUD + TS routing). Code review fixes applied: stale notification bug, disconnected feedback, symlink safety.

title: Make web tool rate limits configurable
description: Add web_search_limit and web_fetch_limit to config system, apply to main agent and subagents via IPC
summary: Added web_search_limit, web_fetch_limit, web_subagent_search_limit, web_subagent_fetch_limit to LimitsConfig (config_loader.py). Main agent reads them in _register_tools(). set_limits() updates the live RunBudget immediately. SubprocessInput carries web limits over IPC. runner.py applies a RunBudget from input_data. delegation.py reads _limits from main_agent and forwards subagent limits. config_handler.py get/save both include the four fields. protocol.ts LimitsData extended. state.ts initialState updated. AutoApprovePanel.tsx has four number inputs. index.css has aa-budget-label. Test fixtures updated. TypeScript clean, 514 Python tests pass, 8 pre-existing TS test failures unchanged.

title: Slice 1: Python config — PromptEnrichmentConfig
summary: Added PromptEnrichmentConfig dataclass to config_loader.py, wired into LLMConfigData, load_llm_config, save_llm_config. Exposed in config_handler.py get/save.

title: Fix 4 code review issues + compile enrichment feature
description: Fix: history forwarding, error display, handleSend with preview, protocol type. Then compile.
summary: Fixed 4 code review issues: (1) history forwarding in sidebar-provider.ts, (2) enrichmentError now shows error to user in App.tsx, (3) handleSend reordered to use enriched preview text when active, (4) enrichmentLoading blocks send. Added types.ts fix. Added 10 enrichment unit tests (49 passing). Compiled both webview (dist/webview.js) and extension (out/extension.js) — both contain streaming delta handlers and history forwarding.

title: Create src/prompts/enrichment.py
summary: Created src/prompts/enrichment.py with ENRICHMENT_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant.

title: Add file nodes (layer 4) to knowledge DB
description: Scan all ~224 Python files and add as type='file' nodes with role descriptions. Link to parent modules via 'contains' edges and to components via 'defines' edges.
summary: 227 Python files added as layer 4 nodes with docstring descriptions and module containment edges.

title: Split briefing into compact + detailed
description: Compact overview (~1500 tokens) for system prompt auto-injection. Detailed module/component info available via query tools.
summary: Split into render_compact_briefing() + render_module_detail/file_detail/search/impact. Compact is ~1500 tokens for system prompt.

title: Python: Reverse loader priority - project .clarity/agents/ overrides built-ins
description:
Currently SubAgentConfigLoader.discover_all() explicitly blocks project .clarity/agents/ files from overriding built-in Python constants. Reverse this so project-level configs take highest priority (for fork/override support).

Files: src/subagents/config.py
- Remove the `if name not in builtin_names` guard for project_dir configs
- Add a `source` field to metadata: "builtin" | "project" | "user"
- Update docstring to reflect new priority order: project > builtin > user
summary: Reversed loader priority in SubAgentConfigLoader.discover_all(). Load order is now: user global (lowest) -> built-in -> project .clarity/agents/ (highest). Project configs can now override built-ins by name. Stamped source field ("builtin"/"project"/"user") into metadata for all loaded configs. _load_from_directory() accepts source param.

title: Python: Add subagent CRUD server messages
description:
Add new JSON-RPC message handlers to stdio_server.py and ws_protocol.py:

Client -> Server:
  - list_subagents: {} -> returns subagents_list
  - save_subagent: {name, description, system_prompt, tools?, is_fork?} -> subagent_saved
  - delete_subagent: {name} -> subagent_deleted
  - reload_subagents: {} -> subagents_list (hot-reload)

Server -> Client responses:
  - subagents_list: {subagents: [{name, description, system_prompt, tools, source: "builtin"|"project", config_path?}]}
  - subagent_saved: {success, name, message}
  - subagent_deleted: {success, name, message}

Also update config_handler.py: replace static SUBAGENT_NAMES list with dynamic load from SubAgentConfigLoader.

Files: src/server/stdio_server.py, src/server/ws_protocol.py, src/server/config_handler.py, src/subagents/config.py
summary: Added list_subagents, save_subagent, delete_subagent, reload_subagents handlers to stdio_server.py. Registered all 4 in _HANDLERS dispatch table. Handlers use SubAgentConfigLoader directly for list/reload, write .md files to .clarity/agents/ for save, unlink for delete. All handlers hot-reload subagent_manager and refresh delegation tool description if agent is available. Also updated test_builtin_subagents.py to allow source='project' for forked built-ins. 188/188 tests passing.

title: TypeScript: Add subagent types to protocol
summary: Added SubAgentInfo interface to shared/protocol.ts. Added subagents_list, subagent_saved, subagent_deleted to ServerMessage union. Added list_subagents, save_subagent, delete_subagent, reload_subagents to ClientMessage in types.ts. Added listSubagents, saveSubagent, deleteSubagent, forkSubagent, openSubagentFile to WebViewMessage. Re-exported SubAgentInfo from types.ts. TypeScript compile shows only pre-existing errors unrelated to our changes.

title: POC: Beads as sole task management system

summary: Added last_stop_reason, current_todo_id, _error_budget_resume_count, _successful_tools_since_resume as plain attrs in __init__. Reset them in reset_session(). Removed TaskState import and instantiation from both. Removed todos_path/set_persistence_path from set_session_id().

title: Fix code review issues from TaskState migration
description: Address all critical and important issues found by code reviewer: resume metadata not applied, stale current_todo_id, task_get in SILENT_TOOLS, double DB query in todo_state, plus suggestions (id field, SQL filter, SQLite timeout, helper factoring).
summary: Fixed all critical and important review issues: (1) resume now applies last_stop_reason/current_todo_id from hydrator, (2) current_todo_id derived live via _get_in_progress_bead_id() helper, (3) task_get removed from SILENT_TOOLS and tool_schemas comment, (4) todo_state cached in local var at both call sites, (5) id field added to _get_bead_todos(), (6) SQLite timeout=5.0 added, (7) in-progress lookup factored into _get_in_progress_bead_id(), (8) SQL filtering via get_all_beads(status=) instead of Python filter.

title: Python: Hot-reload subagents after save/delete
description:
When a subagent is saved or deleted via the server, the running agent instance must reload its SubAgentConfigLoader so the next delegation picks up the changes without restarting.

- SubAgentManager (src/subagents/manager.py) needs a reload() method
- CodingAgent needs to expose this to the server after a save_subagent or delete_subagent response
- Also: SubAgent.create_template() already exists on SubAgentConfig - wire it up for the fork operation

Files: src/subagents/manager.py, src/core/agent.py, src/server/stdio_server.py
summary: Added refresh_description() public method to DelegateToSubagentTool (delegation.py) — assigns self.description from _generate_description(), logs the refresh. Extracted _reload_subagents_and_refresh_tool() helper on StdioProtocol — calls manager.reload_subagents() then delegation_tool.refresh_description(). Replaced 3 identical inline hot-reload blocks in save/delete/reload handlers with single helper call. Fixed dangling else syntax error from refactor. 212/212 tests passing.

title: TypeScript: Wire subagent messages in sidebar-provider.ts
description:
Add message routing in claraity-vscode/src/sidebar-provider.ts:

handleWebviewMessage() new cases:
  - listSubagents -> send list_subagents to server
  - saveSubagent -> send save_subagent to server
  - deleteSubagent -> send delete_subagent to server
  - forkSubagent -> send save_subagent with is_fork=true
  - openSubagentFile -> open .clarity/agents/<name>.md in VS Code editor (like mcpOpenConfig pattern)

handleServerMessage() new cases:
  - subagents_list -> postToWebview
  - subagent_saved -> postToWebview + trigger reload
  - subagent_deleted -> postToWebview

File: claraity-vscode/src/sidebar-provider.ts
summary: Added message routing in sidebar-provider.ts handleWebviewMessage() for: listSubagents, saveSubagent, deleteSubagent, forkSubagent, openSubagentFile. forkSubagent maps to save_subagent with is_fork=true. openSubagentFile opens the .md file in VS Code editor with a warning if no project-level file exists yet. Also fixed pre-existing approveKnowledge TS error by casting to any. TypeScript now compiles with 0 errors.

title: VS Code UI: Subagents panel in sidebar
description:
Add a subagents management panel to the sidebar HTML in getInlineHtml() (sidebar-provider.ts).

Layout:
  - Toolbar icon (codicon-extensions or codicon-person) to toggle the panel
  - Panel sections: "Built-in" (collapsed, read-only cards) + "Custom" (expanded, editable)
  - Each built-in card: name, description, "Fork" button
  - Each custom card: name, description, "Edit" / "Delete" / "Open File" buttons
  - "New Subagent" button at top of Custom section
  - Inline editor form (slides in): name input, description input, system prompt textarea, tools checklist, Save/Cancel buttons

Behavior:
  - Panel opens -> sends listSubagents -> renders cards
  - Fork: pre-fills form with built-in data, saves as project-level copy
  - Delete: confirmation prompt before sending deleteSubagent
  - Open File: opens .md file in VS Code editor for power users

CSS: follow existing panel pattern (#config-panel, #jira-panel)
Icons: ALWAYS use codicons (codicon-extensions, codicon-edit, codicon-trash, codicon-copy, codicon-file-code)

File: claraity-vscode/src/sidebar-provider.ts (getInlineHtml() section)
summary: Completed SubagentsPanel React component with: checkbox-based tool selection (availableTools from server), stale notification fix (onClearNotification called in handleNew/handleEdit/handleFork), disconnected save/delete feedback (extension host returns immediate error if no connection), symlink safety check on delete (resolve + is_relative_to), CSS for tools-checklist, App.tsx wiring. TypeScript clean, reducer tests 121/121 passing, Vite build clean.

title: Configurable tool loop limits (toggle + thresholds)
description: Add user-configurable limits for tool call budget and error budget. Each limit has an enable/disable toggle (checkbox) and threshold inputs that appear only when enabled. Config stored in config.yaml limits section. UI in VS Code settings panel. Defaults: tool_call_limit_enabled=true/max_tool_calls=200, error_budget_enabled=true/error_budget_threshold=3/max_error_budget_resumes=2. When disabled, the corresponding checks in stream_response and tool_gating are skipped entirely.
summary: Implemented configurable limits: LimitsConfig dataclass in config_loader.py, get/set_limits on CodingAgent, get_limits/save_limits handlers in stdio_server via config_handler, LimitsData protocol type, LimitsPanel React component with toggle+threshold UI, full state/actions/reducer/dispatch wiring. Tests updated for agent refactored attribute names.

title: Multimodal tool results for document image extraction
description: Extend ToolResult pipeline to support images. 6 files: agent.py (_frame_tool_result), memory_manager.py (add_tool_result signature), message.py (create_tool signature), base.py (validate_messages), openai_backend.py (cache control for list content), anthropic_backend.py (tool result translation). document_extractor.py needs image extraction via PyMuPDF page.get_images() and python-docx inline_shapes. Scoping complete - see session for full trace.
summary: Implemented multimodal tool results pipeline. 6 files changed: document_extractor.py (image extraction for PDF/DOCX), file_operations.py (multimodal content building), agent.py (_frame_tool_result for list content), memory_manager.py + message.py (type hints), anthropic_backend.py (tool_result translation). 13 new tests, 44 total pass, 0 regressions.

title: Unify tool schemas: wire _get_parameters to tool_schemas.py
description: All tool classes have hardcoded _get_parameters() disconnected from tool_schemas.py. ReadFileTool was fixed (delegates to READ_FILE_TOOL.parameters). Apply same pattern to WriteFileTool, EditFileTool, AppendToFileTool, ListDirectoryTool, GrepTool, GlobTool, RunCommandTool, and knowledge tools. Single source of truth prevents schema drift. Trade-off: richer descriptions cost more tokens per LLM call -- consider parameters_brief variant if token budget becomes a concern.
summary:
Holistic tool schema unification complete. All 5 slices done plus code review findings addressed:
- Fixed 38 remaining path= calls in test_search_tools.py (were passing accidentally via monkeypatch.chdir)
- Added explicit path= rejection in GrepTool and GlobTool execute() to convert silent ignore into error
- Corrected TestNoInlineSchemasDiverge docstring and scope (was misleadingly named 'any Tool subclass')
- Added TestRegistryIntegrity: duplicate name detection + registry/ALL_TOOLS size parity check
- 47/47 tests pass. Follow-up bd-146dda6b tracks file_operations migration.

title: Live run_command output streaming in ToolCard
description: Show live stdout/stderr in the VS Code ToolCard while run_command is executing, instead of waiting for completion. Requires 4-layer change: (1) Switch subprocess.run to Popen with async line-by-line reads in file_operations.py, (2) Emit periodic update_tool_state() with partial output during execution in agent.py, (3) Protocol already supports merging multiple tool_state_updated events, (4) ToolCard.tsx needs to render result content while status is still running. Throttle updates to ~500ms to avoid flooding websocket. Handle Windows Popen quirks and stdin=DEVNULL constraint.

title: Trace Panel Polish
description: Series of UX enhancements to the TracePanel (TracePanel.tsx) -- the animated agent pipeline visualization in the VS Code sidebar. Enhancements identified during the 2026-04-12 session.

title: Trace Panel: resizable detail side panel
description: Add drag handle on the left edge of the detail panel so user can resize it. Width persisted to localStorage under key claraity.tracePanel.sideWidth. No min/max clamp. Transition disabled during drag via isDraggingSide state.
summary: Implemented in TracePanel.tsx. sideWidth state + isDraggingSide + onDragHandleMouseDown callback. Drag handle is 4px strip on left edge of detail panel, highlights on hover. Built successfully.

title: Trace Panel: click Tools node to see available tools
description: When user clicks the Tools SVG node in TracePanel, fetch the current tool list from Python (native + MCP) and display it in the detail panel. Name + description shown by default, collapsible JSON schema per tool. On-demand fetch via getToolList message -- always fresh, picks up MCP tools added mid-session.
summary: All 4 slices implemented and working. Root cause of initial "loading" issue was dev mode using compiled binary -- switching to source mode resolved it. Debug logs added and removed. Clean build confirmed.
design: Slice 1: Python stdio_server.py handle get_tool_list, serialize agent._get_tools(). Slice 2: sidebar-provider.ts forward getToolList/tool_list messages. Slice 3: App.tsx store toolList in state, pass to TracePanel. Slice 4: TracePanel UI render tool list with collapsible schemas.

title: ClarAIty README -- Educational Journey
description: Revamp the README to tell the story of how a raw LLM chat completion evolves into a full AI coding agent. Target audience: beginners and non-programmers. Structure: problem/solution per concept, anchored to the trace panel diagram. Each section backed by actual code exploration -- no guessing.

title: README Step 1: LLM Backend -- the raw API call
description: Explore src/llm/ to understand the raw LLM API call. Document: what a chat completion request looks like, what parameters matter, what comes back. This is the seed of everything -- the simplest possible thing before any agent logic is added.
summary: Explored src/llm/base.py. Drafted Chapter 1 covering: raw chat completion primitive, the 3 roles, key parameters, LLMResponse structure, two-provider abstract interface, and what's missing (no memory, no actions, no judgement). Saved to .claraity/readme-drafts/01-llm-backend.md

title: README Step 2b: Memory System -- where knowledge lives
description: Document the full memory system: 4-level file hierarchy (enterprise/user/project/imports), agent-managed persistent memory (.claraity/memory/), MEMORY.md as index, what agent chooses to save, @import system, why markdown over database. Separate chapter from Context Builder.
summary: Explored src/memory/file_loader.py and memory_manager.py. Drafted Chapter 2b covering: 2 kinds of memory, 4-level hierarchy with OS paths, agent-managed .claraity/memory/ structure, MEMORY.md index pattern, what agent saves vs doesn't, @import system with security rules, write scope table, why markdown over database. Saved to .claraity/readme-drafts/02b-memory-system.md

title: Disconnected UX: show server-offline state across all webview panels
description: When server is not connected, webview panels show misleading states: Settings Save/Fetch unresponsive, MCP Marketplace stuck at Searching, Session History shows No sessions found, Subagents shows No built-in subagents. Fix: add shared isConnected state in webview context, show consistent offline banner, disable interactive controls, replace misleading empty states with Server offline message.

title: list_directory and glob silently fail on dot-folders (.claraity)
description: list_directory('.claraity/readme-drafts') returns empty with no error, and glob('**/*.md', file_path='.claraity/readme-drafts') also returns no results -- despite the folder containing 8 files (confirmed via Windows Explorer). read_file works fine with absolute paths into .claraity subfolders. Both tools appear to silently exclude dot-folders from directory traversal. Discovered while trying to list readme draft files in .claraity/readme-drafts/.
summary: Removed blanket dot-prefix check from GrepTool._should_skip and GlobTool._should_skip; expanded skip_dirs with explicit noisy dirs. Replaced filter_paths() in ListDirectoryTool with per-entry is_blocked() so gitignore no longer censors explicitly-listed directories. All 152 tests pass in dev mode.

title: Users cannot cancel run_command tool execution mid-flight
description: When the agent runs a long-running command via run_command (e.g. a build, test suite, or npm install), users have no way to cancel it mid-execution. The interrupt button in the UI stops the LLM stream but the underlying subprocess keeps running to completion. This is especially painful for background=false commands that block the agent loop -- the user is stuck waiting with no escape hatch. Discovered during session while running long build commands.
design: When user hits interrupt during a run_command execution, the agent should: (1) send SIGTERM/SIGKILL to the subprocess, (2) return a ToolResult with status=cancelled and whatever partial output was captured so far. Need to hook into the agent interrupt signal at the subprocess level. RunCommandTool.execute() currently uses subprocess.run() which is blocking -- may need to switch to subprocess.Popen() with a polling loop that checks for interrupt signal, or run in a thread with a cancellation event.
acceptance_criteria:
- User clicks interrupt during run_command -- subprocess is killed within 1-2 seconds
- Partial output captured so far is returned in the tool result
- Agent loop continues normally after cancellation
- background=true commands (already non-blocking) also get killed on interrupt

title: Test reverse flow: agent queries DB for task

title: Add pages parameter to read_file for PDFs
description: Claude Code's Read tool has a pages param for PDF page-range selection. ClarAIty's read_file uses line-based start_line/end_line which works but is less intuitive for PDFs. Add optional pages param that maps to page-level extraction in document_extractor.py.

title: Add image file reading to read_file tool
description: Claude Code's Read tool can read PNG/JPG files and return them as visual multimodal content. ClarAIty's read_file doesn't support image files at all. Blocked by multimodal tool results bead.

title: Context builder vision capability gating
description: Strip multimodal content blocks at context builder layer when target model lacks vision support. Storage always keeps full multimodal content. Mid-session model switch handled transparently. Depends on a supports_vision capability flag on LLMConfig or LLMBackend.

title: Harden agent.py config error detection
description:
During review of the LLM error classification change in agent.py, several minor issues were identified before committing:

1. Duplicate `import re as _re` inside the exception handler (already imported a few lines above in the same block)
2. `_CONFIG_ERROR_SIGNALS` tuple is defined inside the hot path (rebuilt on every error) -- should be a module-level constant
3. `"authentication"` and `"unauthorized"` are broad -- could false-positive if agent is working on auth-related user code that throws a runtime exception
4. `"invalid_request"` catches content policy/moderation refusals from OpenAI, which would show as "API configuration error" instead of a clearer message
5. `recoverable = True` is set identically in all three branches -- the variable adds no value vs the old hardcoded value; either make it meaningful or remove it
summary: Centralized LLM provider error classification into failure_handler.py. Added classify_provider_error(), enriched exception classes with user_message/event_type/retryable, consolidated keyword sets into frozensets, made handle_api_error() delegate to classifier, simplified agent.py except block (removed duplicate imports, dead recoverable variable, inline phrase matching). 22 new tests covering all classification cases including false-positive prevention and Anthropic bytes payload extraction. All 37 targeted tests pass.
design:
- Move `_CONFIG_ERROR_SIGNALS` to module level as a frozenset constant
- Remove duplicate `import re as _re` (keep only the first one, or move both to top of method)
- Tighten phrase list: replace bare "authentication" / "unauthorized" with more specific phrases (e.g. "authentication_error", "unauthorized access to api")
- Handle moderation/content policy as a separate branch with its own user message
- Evaluate whether `recoverable` needs to be a variable at all given all branches are True
acceptance_criteria:
- No duplicate imports in exception handler
- _CONFIG_ERROR_SIGNALS is a module-level frozenset
- No false positives on auth-related user code errors
- Content policy violations show a distinct message, not "API configuration error"
- Code review passes without style concerns

title: Show meaningful error messages for invalid settings panel configuration
description: When a user enters invalid configuration in the VS Code settings panel (e.g., bad API key format, unsupported model name, invalid URL), the UI currently gives no feedback or shows a generic error. Users need clear, actionable messages explaining what is wrong and how to fix it.
design: Validate config fields in the WebView settings form before sending saveConfig to the extension host. Show inline field-level error messages for known bad inputs (empty required fields, malformed URLs, unrecognised model names). For errors caught server-side (e.g., API key rejected by LLM backend), relay the error back via an ExtensionMessage and display it in the settings panel. Follow the Three-Layer Message Bridge pattern: WebView validation -> extension host relay -> Python agent feedback.
acceptance_criteria:
- Invalid fields show inline error messages in the settings panel (not just console errors)
- Messages are specific and actionable (e.g., "API key cannot be empty", "Model name is not recognised")
- Errors from the Python agent (e.g., auth failure) are surfaced back to the settings panel
- Valid config saves without showing any error
- Error state clears when the user corrects the field

title: Agent-writable memory.md (3-tier hierarchy)
description: Currently .claraity/memory.md (and user-level ~/.claraity/memory.md) are developer-authored files loaded by file_loader.py -- the agent cannot write to them. The agent can only write to .claraity/memory/ (persistent memory system via MEMORY.md index). Gap: when a user says 'remember X for this project', the agent should be able to write to .claraity/memory.md (project level) or ~/.claraity/memory.md (user level). The file_loader.py already has a quick_add() method that supports this -- it just needs to be wired up as a tool or agent action.

title: Remove stale analyze_code references from subagent prompts
description: analyze_code is referenced in KNOWLEDGE_BUILDER_TOOLS, EXPLORE_TOOLS, PLANNER_TOOLS allowlists and in the prose of KNOWLEDGE_BUILDER_PROMPT, PLANNER_PROMPT, and GENERAL_PURPOSE_PROMPT -- but the tool no longer exists in src/tools/. When a subagent tries to call it, it will get a tool-not-found error. Discovered while writing Chapter 7 of the educational README.
summary: Removed all analyze_code references from active src/ files: subagents/__init__.py (KNOWLEDGE_BUILDER_TOOLS, EXPLORE_TOOLS allowlists + prose in PLANNER_PROMPT and GENERAL_PURPOSE_PROMPT), plan_mode.py (plan mode allowlist), ui/formatters.py (tool icon map and two tool_name checks), enhanced_prompts.py (tool reference section, pattern examples, prose). Remaining occurrences are in .bak and dated backup files only.
design: Remove analyze_code from all three tool allowlists (KNOWLEDGE_BUILDER_TOOLS, EXPLORE_TOOLS, PLANNER_TOOLS) and replace all prose references with the correct alternatives (read_file, grep, glob). File: src/prompts/subagents/__init__.py lines 880, 1159, 1312, 1578, 1603.
acceptance_criteria: No occurrence of analyze_code anywhere in src/. All subagent prompts reference only tools that actually exist.

title: BG Tasks panel doesn't update to DONE when background task completes
description: When a background task completes, the agent receives the BACKGROUND TASK UPDATE notification and responds in chat, but the BG Tasks panel in the sidebar still shows the task as RUNNING. The run_command tool card correctly shows "success" and the result appears in chat, but the panel badge never transitions from RUNNING to DONE. Observed with bg-4 (pytest run) -- completed in 75s, chat showed results, panel stayed at RUNNING 0s indefinitely.
summary: Not a bug. Panel update mechanism works correctly. RUNNING state was accurate -- trailing pipe (| tail -15) kept subprocess alive after pytest finished. Panel updated to timed_out correctly when timeout fired. Operational fix: never use piped commands (| head/tail/grep) with background=true.
design: The background task completion event needs to flow through to the BG Tasks panel state. Likely the store event or server message that carries the task completion status is not being dispatched to the panel, or the panel is not subscribing to it. Check: (1) what event the Python side emits when a background task completes, (2) whether serializers.py forwards it, (3) whether the webview dispatch.ts handles it and updates the bgTasks state.
acceptance_criteria: When a background task completes, the BG Tasks panel transitions the task from RUNNING to DONE within one render cycle of the completion event arriving.

title: Close file preview tab after edit_file / write_file completes
description: When the agent modifies a file via edit_file or write_file, VS Code opens a diff preview tab (e.g. ".gitignore (edit_file)"). This tab is not closed after the write completes -- it stays open indefinitely, cluttering the editor. User reported this from screenshot showing .gitignore (edit_file) tab remaining open after the edit was committed.
summary: Added closeDiffTab(callId) to sidebar-provider.ts. Called after diffProvider.clear() in the approvalResult handler. Tab now closes automatically on approve/reject. Verified working in dev mode.

title: task_link: support linking multiple tasks in one call
description: Currently task_link only links one from_id to one to_id per call. When the agent needs to link multiple tasks (e.g. discovered-from a parent, related to siblings) it must make N separate calls. Change to_id to accept a comma-separated list of target IDs so multiple links can be created in one call.
design: Change to_id parameter to accept comma-separated IDs (e.g. "bd-a1b2,bd-c3d4"). Loop over them in execute(), call store.add_dependency() for each. Return summary of all links created. Update schema description and tool_schemas.py. Backward compatible -- single ID still works.
acceptance_criteria: - task_link("bd-a", "bd-b,bd-c,bd-d") creates 3 links in one call - single to_id still works unchanged - output lists all links created - schema updated

title: Switch GrepTool backend from Python re to ripgrep (rg)
description: GrepTool currently uses Python re module which has different regex syntax from ripgrep. LLMs are trained on Claude Code which uses ripgrep, causing subtle bugs (e.g. \| instead of | for OR). Switching to rg as subprocess backend would: fix regex syntax parity, improve performance, get native gitignore support, and drop custom FILE_TYPE_MAP/_should_skip/filter_paths logic. Requires bundling rg.exe in claraity-vscode/bin/ alongside the Python binary.
design: 1. Bundle rg binary in claraity-vscode/bin/ and include in PyInstaller spec. 2. Rewrite GrepTool.execute() to build rg subprocess command. 3. Use rg --json output for reliable parsing. 4. Drop _find_files, _should_skip, filter_paths, FILE_TYPE_MAP, validate_regex_safety from GrepTool (rg handles all of these natively). 5. Keep fallback to Python re if rg not found. 6. Update tool schema description to reflect ripgrep syntax.
acceptance_criteria: - grep tool works with ripgrep regex syntax (| for OR, \| also works) - native gitignore respected automatically - FILE_TYPE_MAP replaced by rg --type - all existing grep tests pass or are updated - rg binary bundled in extension bin/ - graceful fallback if rg not available

title: task_create: support creating multiple tasks in one call
description: Currently task_create creates one task per call. When the agent discovers multiple related issues it must make N sequential calls. Support batch creation by accepting an array of task objects in a single call. Each object has the same fields as the current single-create schema.
design: Two options: (A) Add a tasks[] array parameter alongside existing single-task params -- if tasks[] provided, batch create, otherwise single create as today. (B) Always take a tasks[] array. Option A is backward compatible and safer. Each item in tasks[] has same fields as current schema. Return list of created bead IDs and titles. Update tool_schemas.py and BeadCreateTool.execute().
acceptance_criteria: - task_create with tasks=[{...}, {...}] creates multiple tasks in one call - existing single-task calls still work unchanged - output lists all created task IDs - schema updated - deps field works per-task in batch mode

title: Migrate file_operations tools to _SCHEMA_NAME delegation pattern
description: The _SCHEMA_NAME pattern was established in bd-dbbd60fd. Six tools in file_operations.py still have inline _get_parameters() overrides that should delegate to tool_schemas._SCHEMA_REGISTRY instead: ReadFileTool (currently delegates manually via lazy import, not _SCHEMA_NAME), WriteFileTool, EditFileTool, AppendToFileTool, ListDirectoryTool, RunCommandTool. The consistency test TestNoInlineSchemasDiverge already detects these -- just need to add src.tools.file_operations to the scanned modules in the test once all are migrated.
summary:
Migrated all remaining tools to _SCHEMA_NAME delegation pattern:
- file_operations.py: ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, EditFileTool, AppendToFileTool, ListDirectoryTool, RunCommandTool -- all now use _SCHEMA_NAME + pull name/description from _SCHEMA_REGISTRY in __init__
- clarify_tool.py: ClarifyTool
- background_tools.py: CheckBackgroundTaskTool
- checkpoint_tool.py: CreateCheckpointTool
- web_tools.py: WebFetchTool, WebSearchTool
- plan_mode_tools.py: EnterPlanModeTool, RequestPlanApprovalTool
- delegation.py: DelegateToSubagentTool (parameters only -- description stays dynamic)
- Expanded TestNoInlineSchemasDiverge to cover all 8 modules
- Expanded SCHEMA_DELEGATING_TOOLS parametrize list to 11 tools
- ClarifyTool skipped in signature test (kwargs-only by design, documented)
- 73 passed, 1 skipped
design: For each tool: add _SCHEMA_NAME = 'tool_name', remove inline _get_parameters(). ReadFileTool already imports tool_schemas lazily -- just switch to _SCHEMA_NAME. After all 6 are done, add file_operations to TestNoInlineSchemasDiverge._get_all_tool_subclasses().
acceptance_criteria: All 6 file_operations tools use _SCHEMA_NAME. TestNoInlineSchemasDiverge covers file_operations module. All tests pass.

title: Improve grep tool directory auto-detect: return content when result set is small
description: When grep is called on a directory without explicit output_mode, it auto-detects 'files_with_matches' mode. This is correct for large directories (prevents 7MB+ output) but misleading for small directories -- returning only file paths looks like "no useful results" to both the agent and the user. Discovered when searching src/claraity/ for PRAGMA: two file paths were returned instead of the four matching lines, causing the agent to misread the output as empty.
summary: GrepTool FILES_WITH_MATCHES mode now uses COUNT internally per file and formats output as "path (N match/matches)". Output is unambiguous -- agent can no longer misread it as empty. All 152 tests pass in dev mode.
design:
Options to consider:
1. Switch to 'content' mode when matched file count is below a threshold (e.g., <= 5 files).
2. Always append match count per file in 'files_with_matches' output so it's clear matches exist.
3. Add a note to the tool output header clarifying the mode that was used.
Option 2 or 3 is lowest risk (no behavior change, just clarity). Option 1 changes auto-detect logic -- verify it doesn't break the GrepTool invariant in tool_schemas.py.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agent does not misread directory grep output as "no results found"
- Large directory searches still default to files_with_matches (no regression)
- test_schema_consistency.py still passes (GrepTool invariant unbroken)

acceptance_criteria: All task tool actions demonstrated successfully in a single session.

summary: Removed TaskState/TaskCreateTool etc. from tools/__init__.py imports and __all__. Deleted src/tools/planning_tool.py and src/tools/task_state.py. Fixed stale comment in subagents/runner.py. All imports clean.

content: After reload_subagents(), must also refresh the DelegateToSubagentTool description. The LLM reads tool.description fresh on every call (openai_backend.py:560, anthropic_backend.py:390), so the fix is: add refresh_description() to DelegateToSubagentTool (delegation.py) that re-runs _generate_description() and assigns to self.description. The hot-reload handler calls both: subagent_manager.reload_subagents() then delegation_tool.refresh_description(). Agent holds both references so this is a two-liner.
author: agent

content:
Implementation steps:
1. config_loader.py - Add LimitsConfig dataclass (5 fields + defaults)
2. agent.py constructor - Accept limits config, store as self._limits
3. agent.py stream_response (line 1615-1618) - Replace hardcoded locals with self._limits.* behind _enabled flag
4. tool_gating.py _check_error_budget - Skip when error_budget_enabled=False
5. config_handler.py - Read/write limits section from config.yaml
6. stdio_server.py - Handle get_limits/save_limits messages
7. protocol.ts - Add message types for limits
8. VS Code settings panel - Add Limits section with toggles + conditional number inputs (follow existing provider panel pattern)

Config shape:
limits:
  tool_call_limit_enabled: true
  max_tool_calls: 200
  error_budget_enabled: true
  error_budget_threshold: 3
  max_error_budget_resumes: 2

Key files: config_loader.py, agent.py, tool_gating.py, config_handler.py, stdio_server.py, protocol.ts, settings panel components

content: Root cause investigated. The panel update mechanism is correct end-to-end (Python callback -> TCP -> sidebar-provider -> webview dispatch -> reducer). The RUNNING state was accurate -- bg-4 was genuinely still running because `| tail -15` held the pipe open after pytest exited. The panel correctly updated to timed_out when the timeout fired. Not a UI bug. Real issue: trailing pipes in background commands keep the subprocess alive indefinitely. Fix: never use | head/tail/grep in background=true commands.
author: agent

content: Implementation complete. GrepTool.execute per-file loop now calls _search_file with COUNT mode internally when effective mode is FILES_WITH_MATCHES, then formats output as "path (N match/matches)". head_limit/offset/metadata["matches"] semantics unchanged. New test test_files_with_matches_includes_count added. Awaiting dev-mode verification by user.
author: agent

content: Implementation complete. Three changes in search_tools.py: removed blanket dot-prefix check from GrepTool._should_skip and GlobTool._should_skip, expanded skip_dirs with .next/.nuxt/.svelte-kit/.turbo/.yarn/.idea/.vscode/.pytest_cache/.mypy_cache/.ruff_cache. One change in file_operations.py: ListDirectoryTool now uses is_blocked() per entry instead of filter_paths() so gitignore no longer censors explicitly-listed directories. Tests updated: test_skips_hidden_directories replaced with test_skips_explicit_skip_dirs; two new list_directory ignore tests added. Awaiting dev-mode verification by user.
